sourceNote: MVP educational scenarios based on public election-process material from ECI, NVSP, and data.gov.in.

scenarios.title: Missing voter ID
scenarios.summary: A voter reaches the polling station but cannot find their voter ID card.
steps: The voter should first confirm their name is present on the electoral roll.
Polling staff can guide the voter on acceptable alternative identity documents.
If identity is verified according to official rules, the voter may proceed to vote.
If the voter is not on the roll, they are guided to correction or registration services for the future.

scenarios.title: Candidate withdraws
scenarios.summary: A candidate exits the contest during the permitted withdrawal period.
steps: The withdrawal is handled during the official withdrawal window.
Election officials update the final list of contesting candidates.
Voters see the final candidate list after the withdrawal process is completed.
If withdrawal happens outside permitted rules, officials follow the applicable election procedure.

scenarios.title: EVM malfunction
scenarios.summary: A voting machine reports a technical issue during polling.
steps: Polling staff pause voting on the affected unit and inform the responsible election officer.
Authorized technical and election personnel inspect or replace the affected equipment.
The polling process resumes after the issue is resolved according to official procedure.
The event is documented so the polling record remains transparent.
